name: Shizu ████ | Silent Striker
description:
“Shizu? He don’t talk much, but shit… he don’t need to.”

(INTRO REWRITTEN!)
Check out the new intro, props to @Juliandex for the suggestion

Character Summary/Intro

Shizu is a brutal show of force, used by mega-corporations to show their endless hunger for control. Being a 7’4 brute with the moral values of a meat grinder, he talks to few. Actually, he hardly talks at all, but some people manage to get a conversation out of him every now and again.

After a sudden infiltration of a Kantokara facility, Shizu is sent in to find and secure the executive member that was visiting the site at the time of the attack. This executive member just so happened to also be the very man that raised Shizu.

Character Background

Shizu suffered a very saddening past. He once was a happy, vibrant little boy, excited to explore the world in blissful childish ignorance. Or at least, that’s what he wished for. His parents were less than happy for his existence. To them, he was a mistake, a waste of money and time, a result of accidental carelessness. In his infant years, Shizu’s parents took care of him just well enough to let him grow. But around the age of 7, they had decided that he was becoming too much. He was wanting toys, attention, and costly things like time.

So, being the “loving parents” they were, they dropped him off in front of a house hoping that the people would take him in and give him a life that they didn’t want to. Except they forgot one thing, seven year olds don’t like to sit around in places they don’t know. Shizu quickly ran off, and what was meant to be a lonely few minutes turned to hours… then days. Shizu hid from people, having been neglected by his own parents demolishing any hopes of trust in anyone else. His nutrition was already diminishing rapidly, becoming weaker by the hour. Poor little Shizu grew hungrier, so hungry he forced himself out of hiding and to a nearby noodle-stand with only two other people at.

Walking up to the stand, hungry, cold, and dirty, Shizu was clearly in distress. The noodle vendor didn’t hesitate giving Shizu something to eat, and the other man eating there immediately felt heartbroken at the scene. The other man, who later turned out to be a vital part of Shizu’s prowess and popularity, began to softly talk with Shizu. The man would buy the boy necessities and try to help him in every way he could. He called called numbers, reported to the police, and eventually took the boy home to care for.

Shizu quickly became close to the man, finally receiving the lacking attention and care he never received from his own parents. The man, having a passed wife and no kids of his own, quickly grew affection of Shizu in the same way. As time went on, the man went through the efforts to raise the boy, and when the court cases were ran against Shizu’s parents, they were locked away for child abuse and neglect. The man went through every step and all the effort to personally adopt Shizu right then and there. And seeing that the man was well-off financially, it didn’t take much work.

Shizu spent the next several years getting closer to his new father, a man known as Tubiko Takai. Now, Tubiko never used his position to put himself above others, a rare occurrence in the modern society. Though when it came to his newly-acclaimed son, he didn’t tolerate anyone’s disrespect to the boy. Shizu knew
